summary: Extract Figma's entire design philosophy across 8 facets, then synthesize into an ArchDisc design brief

result.thesis: Generative AI already learned to write, draw, and film — but the world we actually live in is made of matter, and matter has been locked behind CAD seat-licenses, jargon, and years of training. ArchDisc breaks that lock. It is the beginning of creative AI for the physical world: you describe an object in a sentence, and Archie — a 30B vision-language model running entirely on your own Apple-Silicon machine, free and private — plans it, builds it, and verifies it against a real native geometry kernel until it is genuinely manufacturable. Studio turns that intent into full 3D creation; Forge turns it into tolerance-true mechanical CAD. In Figma's terms, ArchDisc eliminates the gap between imagination and reality for real, buildable things, and makes a formerly credentialed craft accessible to anyone who can describe an idea. The AI lowers the floor so everyone can make; the human raises the ceiling with intent and taste. This page is not a funnel — it is a manifesto and a live proof, a single cinematic scroll where a line of language becomes a rotating, real, manufacturable part before your eyes. Vibe designing for the physical world starts here.

philosophyPillars.name: Collapse the gap — imagination to manufacturable matter
philosophyPillars.statement: The page's one job is to dramatize the shortest possible path from a sentence to a real, buildable object. Lead with the transformation itself, never a feature list. The hero literally enacts 'Describe it. Archie builds it.' — text morphing into rotating manufacturable geometry.
philosophyPillars.whyItMatters: This is Figma's founding mission ('eliminate the gap between imagination and reality') applied to physical matter. Foregrounding the collapse of the gap — not the tooling or workflow — is what makes the claim feel civilizational rather than like a CAD product tour.

philosophyPillars.name: A gated craft, opened to everyone
philosophyPillars.statement: Frame ArchDisc as opening manufacturable CAD/3D — a jargon-heavy, license-locked, years-of-training craft — to anyone who can describe an idea in plain language. Copy stays universal and human, never expert-gated. Free, local, private reinforces 'no gate.'
philosophyPillars.whyItMatters: Figma's 'design is accessible to all / design is bigger than design.' The bigger, more universal the promise, the more it reads as a founding movement instead of a niche tool for engineers.

philosophyPillars.name: AI lowers the floor; the human raises the ceiling
philosophyPillars.statement: Credit the human's sentence and taste as the creative act; cast Archie as the faithful floor-raising executor, never the hero. Show a person directing (a sentence, a judgment) and the machine executing with fidelity. Archie is a collaborator — 'my idea → our idea' — not an opaque oracle.
philosophyPillars.whyItMatters: Straight from Config 2026 ('AI lowered the floor, it has not raised the ceiling — you will') fused with Figma's collaborative 'team sport' ethos. It keeps authorship human and dodges the cheap-AI, black-box, replacement framings that undercut a craft claim.

philosophyPillars.name: Craft is the proof — how it works, not just that it works
philosophyPillars.statement: The page's own obsessive finish IS the argument. Real WebGL geometry at high fidelity (true surfaces, edges, tolerances), typographic rigor, and physically-believable motion prove Archie makes things that are truly well-made — not generic AI slop. Show a rigorous pipeline (describe → plan → build → verify), never a magic button.
philosophyPillars.whyItMatters: Figma stakes everything on craft as the line 'between automation and artistry' and warns that the biggest AI mistake is believing one prompt yields production-ready output. Visible rigor and kernel-true verification are how we earn the frontier claim credibly.

philosophyPillars.name: Ruthless hierarchy, revealed over time
philosophyPillars.statement: One dominant focal point per viewport; the manifesto claim is seen first. Progressive disclosure via scroll — one idea per beat, tight proximity within a beat, generous space between. The single persistent geometry object transforms through states (sentence → wireframe → solid → part) rather than hard-cutting between images.
philosophyPillars.whyItMatters: Figma's 'if everything looks the same, you see nothing,' its Time/Continuity principles, and Smart-Animate's shared-element continuity. It turns a scroll into a legible narrative spine instead of a wall of competing sections and CTAs.

philosophyPillars.name: Bold, dark, immersive maximalism — with restraint
philosophyPillars.statement: Commit to a confident dark, depth-driven, 3D-forward aesthetic with luminous accents, bold kinetic type, and engineering-HUD detailing — the medium demonstrates the message (a physical-world 3D AI). But keep the motion vocabulary tight and every effect motivated; restraint is the differentiator.
philosophyPillars.whyItMatters: Figma's 2026 web trends (real 3D/WebGL, kinetic type, dark mode) meet its motion ethos that unmotivated animation is 'just a filter on top of a design' and that people recognize good motion when it's crafted, not stacked.

philosophyPillars.name: A manifesto, not a funnel — mission over transaction
philosophyPillars.statement: Write at decade-scale and civilizational altitude. No waitlist, no email capture, no fake scarcity, no dark patterns. The hierarchy climaxes on the declaration — 'the beginning of creative AI for the physical world' — not on a form. Any single CTA is honest and reversible (watch Archie build / see the apps).
philosophyPillars.whyItMatters: Figma's 'Play,' decade-long compounding, and ethical-design stance. The deliberate absence of a funnel is itself the signal that this is a founding declaration, and it keeps the brand trustworthy and inclusive by default.

colorTokens.name: --color-paper (void base)
colorTokens.usage: Dominant ~60% surface: the deep navy-black canvas the entire scroll floats in. Never pure #000. Kept from existing brand for continuity.

colorTokens.name: --color-paper-2 (raised void)
colorTokens.usage: First elevation for sections that need separation from base; subtle depth under the 3D canvas and HUD panels.

colorTokens.name: --color-surface
colorTokens.usage: ~30% secondary tier: cards, spec panels, code-to-geometry blocks, Studio/Forge app tiles.

colorTokens.name: --color-surface-2
colorTokens.usage: Second raised elevation for layered HUD chrome, hovered cards, inset readouts — builds immersive depth.

colorTokens.name: --color-clay (electric blue accent)
colorTokens.usage: The single luminous ~10% accent. Reserved for the hero verb emphasis, live-geometry key edges/glow, active pipeline states, and the lone CTA. Signature brand hue; spend it only where it matters.

colorTokens.name: --color-clay-deep
colorTokens.usage: Accent pressed/gradient-low and edge-glow falloff; deeper end of the accent value ramp.

colorTokens.name: --color-clay-soft
colorTokens.usage: Italic Instrument-Serif accent words and low-emphasis luminous text; the accent at high value, low commitment.

colorTokens.name: --color-spark (warm generative accent)
colorTokens.usage: NEW. A single warm ember used ONLY at the generative instant (the moment a sentence resolves into geometry) — Figma's warm=energy/spark against a cool trust palette. Tiny surface area; must pass 3:1 as large graphic, never body text.

colorTokens.name: --color-ink (primary text)
colorTokens.usage: Primary headline/display text. Soft near-white (not #FFF) to reduce halation on dark; verified >=7:1 on paper (AAA).

colorTokens.name: --color-ink-soft (body)
colorTokens.usage: Body copy and lead paragraphs; ~9:1 on paper, comfortably above AA 4.5:1.

colorTokens.name: --color-ink-mid (secondary)
colorTokens.usage: Secondary/supporting copy and captions; ~5.4:1, holds AA for normal text.

colorTokens.name: --color-muted (labels)
colorTokens.usage: Mono HUD labels, section eyebrows, spec keys. ~3.3:1 — use ONLY at large/label sizes or non-essential text, never for small essential body.

colorTokens.name: --color-line
colorTokens.usage: Hairline dividers, grid rules, panel borders — the quiet blueprint scaffolding.

colorTokens.name: --color-line-strong
colorTokens.usage: Emphasized borders, active panel edges, HUD frame lines, focus-ring base tint.

colorTokens.name: --focus-ring
colorTokens.usage: NEW semantic. Dedicated visible focus outline (2px + 2px offset) on every interactive element; distinct from accent so keyboard focus never gets lost against a glowing edge. >=3:1 on all surfaces.

typeScale.spec: Geist Sans 600, clamp(2.9rem,0.8rem+9vw,9rem), line-height 0.98, tracking -0.03em. Reserved for 'Describe it. Archie builds it.' and the closing declaration. Variable-weight / per-glyph motion applies HERE ONLY.
Geist Sans 600, clamp(2.4rem,1.1rem+5.6vw,5.5rem), lh 1.02, tracking -0.028em. One per section beat.
Geist Sans 600 — H1 clamp(2.05→3.9rem) lh1.04; H2 clamp(1.75→2.9rem) lh1.06; H3 clamp(1.2→1.6rem) lh1.18. Big display gets tight leading; steps are deliberate, no in-between sizes.

Instrument Serif italic 400, matched to host line, color --color-clay-soft, tracking -0.005em. One or two emphasis words per beat max (e.g. 'the *physical* world') — sparing, per Figma's decorative-legible rule.
Geist Sans 400, clamp(1.125rem,1.02rem+0.45vw,1.4rem), lh 1.58. Sub-headline framing under a section opener.
Geist Sans 400, clamp(0.9375rem→1.0625rem), lh 1.68, measure capped 40–60ch. Color --color-ink-soft. Generous leading per Figma readability guardrails.
Geist Mono 500, 0.6875rem, tracking +0.2em, UPPERCASE, --color-muted. Engineering-HUD eyebrows and section indices; tracking added because all-caps. Never body length.
Geist Mono, 0.8125rem, tabular-nums + slashed-zero (font-feature 'tnum','zero'). Tolerances, dimensions, benchmark figures, pipeline-state readouts — the blueprint/HUD voice.
Geist Sans 600, clamp(2.6rem→5rem), lh 0.98, tabular-nums slashed-zero. Big proof numbers (e.g. on-device model size, benchmark score).

motionLanguage: Curves: default ease-out-soft = cubic-bezier(0.22,1,0.36,1) for everything ARRIVING (it lands, not stops). Ease-in for anything leaving. Ease-in-out only for reposition-in-place. Never linear for UI — reserve linear strictly for continuous 3D auto-rotation.

Springs over bezier for the hero build: use the 'motion' library spring with low stiffness + higher mass (Figma's 'Slow' register) so the manufactured part scales/rotates in with weighty, believable momentum — signals real physical geometry, not UI flourish.

Personality = calm and considered, not snappy. The manifesto's gravity calls for long, gradual decelerations. Reserve exactly ONE deliberate delight beat (a subtle spring overshoot at the generative instant); bounce is never a default.

Choreograph the hero as a physical story: anticipation (a held beat before) → primary build (geometry materializes) → follow-through (secondary settle/rotation after). Dramatizes Archie's probabilistic-plan → deterministic-build thesis.

Scroll is a continuity engine: ONE persistent geometry object transforms through states (sentence → wireframe → solid → verified part) via scroll progress, not hard cuts between hero images. Sections hand velocity to the next — momentum-preserving beats.

Restraint: a tight, reused vocabulary — one arrival ease, one hero spring, one glow-pulse, one signal-dot pulse. No effect-stacking. Unmotivated motion reads as a filter; every animation carries narrative.

Reduced-motion: prefers-reduced-motion disables auto-rotation, parallax, atmos-breathe, kinetic type, and scroll-driven camera — degrading to static high-fidelity stills and instant opacity transitions. The story must be fully legible with zero motion. Never gate comprehension behind animation.

Performance budget: 3D confined to its own canvas layer; pause rendering when offscreen (IntersectionObserver); target 60fps and cap DPR; motion.library transforms on compositor-friendly properties (transform/opacity) only.
designSystem.spacingAndGrid: One 8pt base spacing scale as CSS custom properties; all section padding, gaps, and stack rhythm derive from it — systematic, never ad-hoc. Layout is a shared 12-column grid inside a max-width container (~1200–1280px) with consistent gutters, honored across every breakpoint so the reveal stays coherent desktop→mobile. Composition is intentionally ASYMMETRIC (Figma's dynamic/kinetic balance): offset the heavy 3D mass against generous negative space and a smaller high-contrast headline block rather than dead-centering — momentum and forward energy. Place hero geometry and headline on rule-of-thirds intersections. White space is load-bearing: vast dark emptiness isolates the hero so it reads as a spotlight reveal; uniform generous section padding; 'a little goes a long way.' Radii from existing scale (sm 8 / 14 / lg 22 / xl 30). Token architecture is layered primitive → semantic → component (e.g. #6d8cff → --color-clay → --hero-title-glow) as one canonical :root source of truth in globals.css, extending the existing @theme block — name tokens for intent (--accent-forge, --depth-fog, --motion-reveal-ease), never literal values. The wide 3D canvas and any spec tables scroll inside their own overflow-x containers so the page body never scrolls horizontally.

sectionIntent.goal: Orient without competing with the reveal; establish brand and the two-apps-one-model fact quietly.
sectionIntent.designMoves: Thin transparent bar over the void; wordmark left, mono links (Studio · Forge · Archie) right, no CTA button crowding the hero. Fades to a faint blurred backing only after first scroll. Full keyboard nav, visible --focus-ring on every link.
sectionIntent.copyDirection: Just the wordmark and three product names. No slogans. Let the hero carry the message.

sectionIntent.goal: In one viewport, enact 'Describe it. Archie builds it.' — a sentence becoming real, rotating, manufacturable geometry. This is the whole thesis in ten seconds.
sectionIntent.designMoves: Mega kinetic headline as the textual focal point on a rule-of-thirds line; live WebGL geometry as the visual focal point, offset asymmetrically against vast negative space. A typed sentence (with mono caret) resolves — one warm --color-spark pulse at the instant — into an accent-edged solid part that settles with a Slow spring and slow auto-rotation. Only the accent glows; everything else defers. Reduced-motion: static high-fidelity render + full text, no typing.
sectionIntent.copyDirection: 'Describe it. Archie builds it.' as the hero. One quiet line beneath: creative AI for the physical world — free, local, private, on your Mac. No feature list. No button fighting the geometry.

sectionIntent.goal: State the civilizational thesis: generative AI gave us words, images, video; ArchDisc extends it into real, manufacturable matter.
sectionIntent.designMoves: Near-empty viewport, symmetrical gravity here (authority), display type isolated in maximal whitespace, one Instrument-Serif italic accent word ('the *physical* world'). A single continuity flowline leads the eye down. Text-only calm after the hero's motion.
sectionIntent.copyDirection: Manifesto voice, decade-scale. 'Text, then images, then video. Now — matter.' Frame it as a beginning, not a product. Universal, not engineer-gated.

sectionIntent.section: 3 · Anyone can build — the gate opens
sectionIntent.goal: Make the promise universal: a formerly credentialed craft is now a sentence away. Human-as-director, Archie-as-collaborator.
sectionIntent.designMoves: Tight Gestalt input→output pairs (plain-language prompt styled alike to its resulting part) so the eye reads transformation instantly. Grouped, grid-anchored, asymmetric. Accent only on the resulting geometry edges. Broad, honest range of use cases (a bracket, a lamp, a gear, an enclosure).
sectionIntent.copyDirection: 'If you can describe it, you can build it.' Credit the human's intent as the creative act; Archie executes with fidelity. No jargon, no prerequisites, no seat-license.

sectionIntent.section: 4 · The system behind the sentence (not a magic button)
sectionIntent.goal: Prove rigor: reveal the repeatable pipeline describe → plan → build → verify against a real geometry kernel. Earn the frontier claim credibly.
sectionIntent.designMoves: The one persistent geometry object advances through states (sentence → wireframe → solid → verified) tied to scroll progress — Smart-Animate continuity, not cuts. Engineering-HUD chrome: mono spec readouts (tolerances, dimensions) in tabular-nums, thin --color-line frames, active-state accent. Each stage is one clearly-grouped beat with widened space between.
sectionIntent.copyDirection: Signal engineering depth without a lecture: probabilistic planning resolving into deterministic, kernel-true geometry. 'Not a lucky one-shot — a verified build.' Truthful about what it does.

sectionIntent.goal: Introduce Studio (full 3D creation) and Forge (mechanical CAD on a native kernel) as two faces of the same intent, powered by Archie on-device.
sectionIntent.designMoves: Two asymmetric surface-tier panels (Studio / Forge) sharing one grid, distinguished by subtle per-section color grading (reuse existing .grade-* system) while the accent stays continuous. A stat row for proof (30B VLM · on-device · near-frontier on CAD-gen). Clear single-focal card each; proximity groups app to its role.
sectionIntent.copyDirection: 'Studio imagines. Forge engineers. Archie runs on your machine.' Emphasize free, local, private, Apple-Silicon-native. State capabilities truthfully — near-frontier, real manufacturable output.

sectionIntent.section: 6 · Closing declaration (the climax, not a form)
sectionIntent.goal: Land the hierarchy climax on the thesis itself. No waitlist, no email, no scarcity.
sectionIntent.designMoves: Second-strongest scale/contrast of the whole page (after hero), isolated in maximal whitespace, the geometry object at rest and fully resolved. Optional single honest, reversible CTA (watch Archie build / open the apps) at low visual weight — the declaration outranks it. Motion decelerates to stillness.
sectionIntent.copyDirection: 'The beginning of creative AI for the physical world.' A founding statement, not a sell. If a CTA exists it is transparent about where it leads. Vibe designing, for real things.

sectionIntent.goal: Close with engineering calm and honest housekeeping.
sectionIntent.designMoves: Mono spec voice, hairline --color-line rules, tabular figures, signal-dot status. Minimal links (privacy, terms, status). Broad, inclusive representation in any imagery.
sectionIntent.copyDirection: Understated. Restate free/local/private once. No re-pitch, no capture, no dark patterns.

donts: No waitlist, no email signup, no 'early access' form, no gated 'request a demo' — anywhere. The page is a manifesto, not a funnel.
No dark patterns: no fake scarcity, countdown timers, manufactured urgency, confirmshaming, pre-checked opt-ins, or forced continuity. Any lone CTA is honest and reversible.
Don't lead with a feature list or spec sheet above the fold — lead with the imagination→matter transformation.
Don't make Archie the hero or an opaque oracle. The human's sentence and taste are the creative act; the machine is the faithful executor and collaborator.
Don't sell a magic-button fantasy — always show the verified describe→plan→build→verify system, or the frontier claim reads as hype.
Never pure #000 background or pure #FFF text; never near-invisible dim-gray body copy for 'mood' — hold body >=4.5:1 (AA) and large display >=3:1 against the void.
Never let color/hue alone carry meaning (build vs complete, active vs idle): pair with icon, label, or shape — and verify against color-blindness simulation.

Don't gate comprehension behind motion: prefers-reduced-motion must yield a fully legible static page (no auto-rotation, parallax, kinetic type, or scroll-camera). Cap DPR and pause offscreen 3D — never tank performance for spectacle.
Don't over-animate: no effect-stacking, no bounce-by-default, no linear UI easing, no motion that isn't narratively motivated (unmotivated motion is 'just a filter').
Don't spend the accent everywhere — one luminous blue plus one tiny warm spark; flooding the page with glow destroys the hierarchy that makes the reveal land.
Don't hard-cut between separate hero images; transform the one persistent geometry object through its states for spatial/narrative continuity.
Don't dead-center everything into static symmetry where the brand wants kinetic momentum; but don't create accidental lopsidedness either — asymmetry must be deliberate and grid-anchored.
Don't crowd the reveal with competing focal points or multiple CTAs — one dominant element per viewport, or 'you see nothing.'
Don't hardcode raw hex/px in components or hand-tune one-off type sizes — every value comes from the layered token system and the modular scale.
Don't speak only to CAD experts or bury the promise in jargon; the invitation is universal — anyone who can describe an idea.
Don't overclaim: state truthfully what Archie can and cannot yet do; manufacturability and kernel-true correctness matter more than the wow, not less.
Don't bolt on accessibility at the end — semantic landmarks, visible focus rings, alt text, and keyboard operability ship from the first commit.
